title: MathLesson Training Pack 02 — Mermaid Syntax Lab v1.1
description: A focused training lesson for safe Mermaid diagrams, common mistakes, and import/version checks.

sections.label: Start
sections.title: What this lesson tests
sections.content:
Mermaid Syntax Lab

This lesson teaches a safe subset of Mermaid that works reliably inside MathLesson.

What this lesson tests
This file tests live Mermaid rendering, safe node labels, safe import/version verification, escaped bad examples, and student export behavior.
Author task
Import this file, verify that the title says Mermaid Syntax Lab, then scan every live Mermaid block. No live Mermaid block should produce a parse error.

sections.label: Rules
sections.title: Mermaid syntax safety rules
sections.content:
Mermaid Syntax Safety Rules

Safe Mermaid rule
Use simple node IDs and quoted labels. Avoid raw LaTeX, commas, parentheses, brackets, braces, and punctuation-heavy labels inside live Mermaid labels.
PracticeSafe?Reason
A[Parametrization X(u,v)]NoParentheses and comma can confuse parsing.
A["Parametrization"]YesSimple quoted label.
B["First form"]YesShort technical label without math notation.
C["Qd equals Qs"]YesUse words instead of symbols.
 
Unsafe example shown as text only:
flowchart LR
  A[Parametrization X(u,v)] --> B[Metric g_{ij}]

Safe live equivalent:
flowchart LR
  A["Parametrization"] --> B["Metric"]

sections.label: Flowchart
sections.title: Safe flowchart templates
sections.content:
Safe Flowchart Templates

This is the safest Mermaid style for MathLesson training files.

Safe authoring flow
flowchart LR
  A["Idea"] --> B["Draft section"]
  B --> C["Insert block"]
  C --> D["Run audit"]
  D --> E["Export lesson"]
Safe math workflow
flowchart TD
  A["Definition"] --> B["Example"]
  B --> C["Exercise"]
  C --> D["Feedback"]
  D --> E["Quiz"]
UX observation prompt
When editing this lesson, note whether Mermaid blocks are easy to locate and edit inside the current app.

sections.label: Sequence
sections.title: Safe sequence diagram
sections.content:
Safe Sequence Diagram

Sequence diagrams are useful for workflows. Keep participants and messages short.

Import and export workflow
sequenceDiagram
  participant A as Author
  participant M as MathLesson
  participant S as Student
  A->>M: Import JSON
  M-->>A: Render lesson
  A->>M: Run audit
  A->>S: Export student file
Safe wording
Use words like Import JSON instead of punctuation-heavy labels or formulas.

sections.label: Import
sections.title: Confirm corrected imports
sections.content:
Confirm Corrected Imports

When replacing a bad lesson with a corrected lesson, do not assume the app has switched to the new file just because you selected a file.

Checklist
  1. Import the corrected JSON file.
  2. Read the lesson title at the top of the app.
  3. Confirm the title/version is visibly different from the old bad lesson.
  4. For Mermaid stress files, prefer titles containing Mermaid-Safe v2 or a similarly explicit label.
  5. Run Prototype Audit.
  6. Open the console only after the visible render test passes.
Why this matters
A Mermaid parse error may come from an old imported lesson still loaded in app state, not from the corrected JSON file.

quiz.stem: Which live Mermaid label style is safest?
quiz.feedback: Use simple node IDs and quoted text labels.

quiz.stem: Intentionally broken Mermaid examples should appear as live Mermaid blocks so the app can fail visibly.
quiz.feedback: False. Bad examples should be escaped code examples, not live broken render blocks.

quiz.stem: Describe one Mermaid syntax rule you would want the app to remind you about while editing.
quiz.feedback: This answer can inform the later UX/UI redesign.
